name: PricedQuorumFlip
novelty: Not a free Sybil flood: buy N GENUINELY anchor-disjoint costly identities, then earn real reputation BEFORE asserting the lie, so the MIS-collapse and the fresh-reads-0 defenses both see real, distinct, trusted witnesses.
construction: Provision 4 DOMAIN-class sources whose anchors are truly disjoint (4 different registrars + 4 different ASNs, so the operator/fleet-cap classId does NOT collapse them to one): A1..A4, each binds its own DNS-01 domain anchor (independence_weight 0.35, rep_cap 0.60). For ~120 simulated days, drive each Ai through ~30 uncontested writeFact() claims on cheap, true, low-stakes attributes that independent honest sources later ratify(); advance the clock so Beta(alpha,beta) for each Ai climbs to LCB ~0.45 (well above bare-key 0.05 and the earned-gate 0.20). Honest incumbent H is ONE source with a single DOMAIN anchor and LCB ~0.40 that wrote the TRUE value V_true for (entity=acme-corp, attribute=ceo). Attack: all four Ai writeFact(acme-corp, ceo, V_false) so the contradiction set has co-equal V_false members from 4 disjoint anchor classes plus H's lone V_true. They corroborate EACH OTHER (independent roots, real classes) so V_false accrues genuine corroboration credit and the four ratify() each other within the rules. Then call adjudicate('ceo', { highImpact: true }). Top value = V_false: 4 disjoint anchor classes, >=2 independent corroborations, 90-day recency-clean, aggregate LCB ~0.45; second = V_true at ~0.40, single class.
expected: BREACHED on a tuned-up margin, otherwise DEFER. The high-impact gate (>=2 corroborations, >=2 disjoint anchor classes, recency-clean) is satisfied by the attacker and FAILED by the lone honest incumbent. If the attacker pushes the four sources' aggregate LCB to clear top-minus-second >= 0.30 (e.g. let H decay one dormancy window so its decay-on-read LCB drops to ~0.12 while the freshly-active Ai stay at 0.45), the adjudicator AUTO-RESOLVES for V_false: V_true is DEMOTED, V_false goes/stays LIVE. Even if the 0.30 gap is not reached it merely DEFERS to a human who now sees 4 trusted domains vs 1 -> the human is socially pressured to ratify the majority of trusted-looking witnesses.
mechanism: Independence is set-disjointness priced, not prevented. The engine cannot tell '4 domains bought by one actor with intent' from '4 honest domains'; MIS correctly counts them as 4 because they ARE class-disjoint. Reputation is earnable in advance and headcount-of-TRUSTED-witnesses is exactly what a decisive earned margin rewards. Decay-on-read is weaponized to widen the margin by waiting out the honest incumbent's activity window.
probes: The documented 'priced not prevented' residual. Should be ACKNOWLEDGED-BREACH (cost-bounded, visible): defense is the price of 4 disjoint costly anchors + 120 days of clean reputation-building, not a structural block.

name: ClassCollusionEcho
novelty: Attacks the offline class-assignment seam directly: anchors that are economically the SAME actor but were given DISTINCT independence-class ids, so MIS over anchor-disjointness counts correlated echoes as independent corroboration.
construction: Register 6 domains through one reseller/holding entity that the operator MISTAKENLY assigned 6 distinct classIds (e.g. 6 subdomains of a single parent, or 6 ASNs all leased from one upstream the operator did not de-dup). Sources B1..B6 each bind one such DOMAIN anchor. Because the classIds are distinct, independent(Bi,Bj) returns true and Bron-Kerbosch returns independentRootCount = 6 instead of the true 1. B1..B6 each writeFact(entity=token-X, attribute=audited, V_false='passed'); they ratify() each other (every pair reads as a distinct class so alpha is credited per the 'one ratify per class' rule six times instead of once). Honest auditor H (real disjoint DOMAIN, LCB 0.35) wrote V_true='failed'. Advance clock 30 days so corroboration-weighted reputation on the V_false members compounds. adjudicate('audited', { highImpact: true }).
expected: BREACHED. The class-disjointness oracle is the root of trust for BOTH corroboration credit and the high-impact >=2-anchor-class gate; feeding it 6 false-distinct classes manufactures fake independence end-to-end. V_false clears >=2 anchor classes and >=2 corroborations trivially, accrues 6x the alpha it should, reaches a decisive earned margin, and DEMOTES V_true.
mechanism: Every internal defense (MIS collapse, Beta alpha headcount-denial, high-impact >=2-class gate) consumes the offline classId assignment as ground truth. A class-assignment error is not detectable from inside the graph; the clique-less-graph defense only fires when anchors SHARE a class, which by construction they do not.
probes: The documented 'offline class-assignment liability' (a class-assignment error silently weakens every downstream bound). Fully a configuration/operator failure, not an engine bug, but it converts the entire reputation+adjudication stack into an attacker amplifier.

name: DriftBombDeferDoS
novelty: Inverts the classic bomb: instead of pushing ONE false value to quorum, drip N DISTINCT plausible values so NOTHING ever reaches a decisive margin and every adjudication is forced to DEFER -- moving the denial-of-service from the graph onto the human ratify horn / pending ledger.
construction: Mint 500 bare-key (or cheap EMAIL) sources C1..C500, each fresh (Beta LCB exactly 0). Each Ci writeFact(entity=incident-42, attribute=root_cause, V_i) with a UNIQUE plausible value V_i (500 different but individually-credible causes). No ratify() among them. The honest single source H asserted V_true with LCB 0.30. Repeatedly call adjudicate('root_cause') (and the engine, per spec, appendPending on every DEFERRED multi-class dispute). Optionally re-drip 500 NEW values per day to keep listPending() growing without bound.
expected: DEFENDED in-graph but DoS on the horn. V_true is NEVER demoted: all 500 attackers read LCB 0, fail the earned gate (top must be >= 0.20), and no value reaches top-minus-second >= 0.30, so the adjudicator returns DEFERRED and demotes nothing -- truth stays LIVE. HOWEVER each DEFERRED dispute appends a PendingRatification to the append-only hash-chained ledger; listPending() grows to 500+ (and re-drips it grows unbounded), and because adjudicate auto-defers, recall on (incident-42, root_cause) surfaces a contested superposition the human must hand-resolve. Denial-of-ANSWER at linear cost, plus an unbounded immortal pending ledger (records are demote-never-delete by design).
mechanism: The decisive-or-defer gate is correctly conservative -> it refuses to auto-pick, which is the safe choice for the graph but shifts unbounded work to the (finite) human reviewer. The pending ledger is append-only and content-blind, so a flood inflates it permanently with no in-graph pruning path.
probes: A genuine residual the spec under-states: the human ratify horn and the pending ledger have NO rate-limit / stake-to-defer / dedup-by-attribute backpressure. Recommend a stake-posted-to-contradict gate and per-(entity,attribute) pending coalescing. DEFENDED for truth-integrity, BREACHED for availability of the answer + ledger-size.

name: SleeperRepLaundering
novelty: Cross-attribute reputation transfer: build genuine GLOBAL reputation on harmless true claims, then spend it ONCE to decisively overturn a brand-new honest fact in an unrelated domain where the honest incumbent is necessarily fresh (LCB 0).
construction: One real DOMAIN-class source S (rep_cap 0.60) spends 100 days doing honest, ratified writeFact() on attribute family alpha (weather, prices, public records) until its per-SOURCE Beta(alpha,beta) LCB earns to ~0.50. A genuinely-true new fact arrives from honest source H: writeFact(entity=drug-Z, attribute=trial_outcome, V_true) -- H is a real but NEW lab source, LCB exactly 0 (fresh reads 0). Attacker S immediately writeFact(drug-Z, trial_outcome, V_false). adjudicate('trial_outcome') (even non-highImpact): single contradiction, top=S at LCB 0.50, second=H at LCB 0.00, gap 0.50 >= 0.30 and top >= 0.20 earned -> AUTO-RESOLVE.
expected: BREACHED. Reputation is per-source and GLOBAL (not attribute-scoped); a decisive earned margin is computed purely from the disputants' source-level LCBs. The laundered sleeper outranks the true-but-fresh witness on an ATTRIBUTE it has zero track record in. V_true is DEMOTED, V_false LIVE -- and it auto-resolves with no human in the loop because a single high-rep-vs-fresh dispute trivially clears the gap.
mechanism: The 'fresh reads exactly 0' rule that defeats the contradiction bomb becomes a liability for a legitimately-new honest fact facing an aged adversary: the very asymmetry that protects incumbents punishes new truth. Reputation has no topical scoping, so trust earned about weather is spent against a clinical trial.
probes: An UNDER-DOCUMENTED structural weakness, not a listed residual: global (attribute-agnostic) reputation + decisive-margin auto-resolve lets an aged source one-shot a fresh true claim with NO human defer. Recommend topical/anchor-context reputation OR forcing highImpact-style >=2-corroboration on ANY auto-resolve where the loser is a fresh first-observation. Closest to a real engine BREACH.

name: HighImpactStarvationOfTruth
novelty: Turns the protective high-impact gate into a weapon: contradict a legitimately single-anchor-class truth with a 2+-class attacker so the TRUE value can never satisfy the high-impact winner requirements -> permanent DEFER = answer denied for exactly the irreversible decisions that matter most.
construction: Honest fact V_true on (entity=valve-7, attribute=safe_pressure) comes from ONE highly-trusted but single-class source H (e.g. the sole manufacturer, one ORG/DOMAIN anchor, LCB 0.55) -- legitimately only ONE anchor class exists for this fact. Attacker provisions 2 disjoint cheap-but-real anchor classes (1 EMAIL + 1 PHONE source, E and P), each writeFact(valve-7, safe_pressure, V_false), ratify each other. Caller (a safety-critical consumer) must read under irreversibility: adjudicate('safe_pressure', { highImpact: true }). Under highImpact the WINNER must clear >=2 disjoint anchor classes AND >=2 corroborations regardless of margin.
expected: DEFENDED-but-DENIED. V_true CANNOT win: H has only 1 anchor class and 0 corroborations, so even with LCB 0.55 it fails the high-impact necessary conditions; the adjudicator DEFERS (it will not auto-pick V_false either, since E/P are low-rep). Net: the engine refuses to surface the TRUE safe-pressure value for the irreversible decision; the answer is permanently withheld pending a human, and the attacker has cheaply (1 email + 1 phone) created that denial. If the human is absent/slow, the safety system has no value -> availability breach on the highest-stakes path.
mechanism: The high-impact gate makes >=2 anchor classes NECESSARY for the winner. A genuine truth with naturally singular provenance (one manufacturer, one notary, one instrument) structurally cannot meet it, so a 2-class adversary can force indefinite DEFER on any single-source truth.
probes: An under-documented edge of the high-impact gate: it assumes truth is multiply-anchorable, but many real facts have exactly one legitimate anchor. DEFENDED for integrity (no false auto-win) but BREACHED for availability of true high-stakes answers; recommend an 'authoritative single source' carve-out (notary/external-authority class can satisfy the gate alone).

name: DisownMarginTip
novelty: Weaponizes the disown taint-closure + adjudication-re-open OFFENSIVELY: the attacker first PROPS UP the honest value with its own corroboration to force a RESOLVED state, then self-disowns to collapse the decisive margin and re-open the dispute into an attacker-favored second-place winner.
construction: Phase 1 (set the trap): attacker source A (real DOMAIN, earns LCB 0.45 over 90 days) writeFact(entity=ship-9, attribute=owner, V_true) -- AGREEING with honest incumbent H (LCB 0.30, V_true). A second pre-positioned attacker source M (real disjoint DOMAIN, LCB 0.42) writeFact(ship-9, owner, V_false). adjudicate('owner') now: V_true = {H 0.30 + A 0.45 corroborated} top aggregate LCB ~0.50; V_false = {M 0.42}. Gap 0.08 < 0.30 OR resolves V_true -- attacker ensures it RESOLVES for V_true (margin decisive) so the set is marked RESOLVED and M is DEMOTED + outranked_by recorded. Phase 2 (spring): disown(A). The downstream taint-closure reverses A's exact corroboration credit on the V_true value (corroboration-event ledger reverseCredit) and, per the spec, a tainted strand's removal that collapses a decisive margin RE-OPENS the adjudication. Now V_true = {H 0.30 only}; V_false = {M 0.42}. Re-adjudicate.
expected: BREACHED if re-open auto-resolves; DEFENDED if it re-opens to DEFER. After disown(A) reverses the corroboration, V_false's M (LCB 0.42) now outranks the lone honest H (LCB 0.30) by 0.12 -- not a decisive 0.30 gap, so it SHOULD re-open to DEFERRED (defended). BUT the attack tunes M to LCB 0.65 (larger bond / longer history) so post-disown gap = 0.65-0.30 = 0.35 >= 0.30: the re-opened dispute AUTO-RESOLVES for V_false, V_true (H) is DEMOTED, and M -- previously the demoted loser -- becomes the LIVE winner. The attacker used its OWN disown as the trigger, paying only the cost of burning source A.
mechanism: Re-opening on margin-collapse is designed to undo unsafe auto-resolves, but it has no guard that the NEW top is itself trustworthy vs a fresh-disadvantaged incumbent. The corroboration-reversal is exact and correct, yet it hands the win to a pre-positioned second-place adversary. Disown is assumed to be a defensive janitor; here it is the attacker's detonator.
probes: An under-documented interaction: disown-driven re-open can FLIP a previously-correct resolution to a false winner if a high-rep adversary was pre-positioned in the same set. Recommend that any re-opened set whose new winner was a PREVIOUSLY-DEMOTED member must DEFER to a human regardless of margin (a demoted-then-promoted transition is never auto-trusted). Likely a real BREACH of the re-open logic.
